6. Sprinkle sesame seeds. Mix everything together before eating.

---

## Egg Fried Rice

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/egg-fried-rice

Simple wok-fried rice with fluffy scrambled eggs and spring onions — fast, delicious, and endlessly adaptable.

**Cuisine:** Asian · **Style:** Rice Bowl · **Protein:** Egg
**Time:** 15 min total (5 prep + 10 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 2 · **Spice:** 1/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Vegetarian · Contains egg · Gluten-free · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 3 cups cold cooked rice
- 3 eggs
- 3 cloves garlic, minced
- 2 tbsp soy sauce
- 1 tbsp sesame oil
- 3 spring onions, chopped
- White pepper
- 2 tbsp oil
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Beat eggs with a pinch of salt.
2. Heat oil in a wok until smoking.
3. Scramble eggs quickly, break into pieces, set aside.
4. Add more oil, sauté garlic briefly.
5. Add cold rice, break up clumps, stir fry on high heat.
6. Add soy sauce, white pepper, and eggs. Toss well.
7. Finish with sesame oil and spring onions.

---

## Tacos

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/tacos

Crunchy corn tortillas loaded with spiced chicken, salsa, and fresh toppings — fun, flavourful, and hands-on.

**Cuisine:** Mexican · **Style:** Bread Meal · **Protein:** Chicken
**Time:** 30 min total (15 prep + 15 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 3/5
**Meal:** dinner · **Dietary:** Non-Veg · Gluten-free

**Ingredients:**
- 400g chicken breast, shredded
- 8 taco shells
- 1 tsp cumin
- 1 tsp paprika
- 1/2 tsp chilli powder
- 1 cup salsa
- 1/2 cup sour cream
- 1 cup shredded lettuce
- 1 tomato, diced
- 1/2 cup shredded cheese
- 1 lime
- 2 tbsp oil
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Season chicken with cumin, paprika, chilli powder, and salt.
2. Cook in oil until done, then shred.
3. Warm taco shells in the oven.
4. Fill shells with chicken, lettuce, tomato, and cheese.
5. Top with salsa and sour cream.
6. Squeeze lime over. Serve immediately.

---

## Burrito Bowl

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/burrito-bowl

A deconstructed burrito with rice, beans, grilled chicken, corn, and all the fixings — hearty and customisable.

**Cuisine:** Mexican · **Style:** Rice Bowl · **Protein:** Chicken
**Time:** 35 min total (15 prep + 20 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 2 · **Spice:** 2/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Non-Veg · Gluten-free

**Ingredients:**
- 2 chicken breasts
- 1.5 cups cooked rice
- 1 cup black beans
- 1/2 cup corn
- 1 avocado, sliced
- 1/2 cup salsa
- 1/4 cup sour cream
- 1 lime
- 1 tsp cumin
- 1 tsp paprika
- Shredded lettuce
- 2 tbsp oil
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Season chicken with cumin, paprika, and salt. Grill or pan-fry.
2. Slice grilled chicken.
3. Cook rice with a squeeze of lime.
4. Warm black beans and corn.
5. Assemble bowls: rice, beans, corn, chicken, lettuce.
6. Top with avocado, salsa, sour cream, and lime.

---

## Falafel Wrap

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/falafel-wrap

Crispy chickpea fritters wrapped in warm pita with tahini, salad, and pickles — a satisfying Mediterranean meal.

**Cuisine:** Mediterranean · **Style:** Bread Meal · **Protein:** Lentils
**Time:** 35 min total (20 prep + 15 cook) · **Difficulty:** medium · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 2/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Vegan · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 1.5 cups chickpeas, soaked overnight
- 1 small onion
- 4 cloves garlic
- 1 cup fresh parsley
- 1 tsp cumin
- 1 tsp coriander powder
- 2 tbsp flour
- 4 pita breads
- 2 tbsp tahini
- 1 tomato, sliced
- Lettuce
- Pickled cucumbers
- Oil for frying
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Blend soaked chickpeas, onion, garlic, parsley, and spices to a coarse paste.
2. Add flour and salt, mix well. Refrigerate 1 hour.
3. Shape into small patties.
4. Deep fry until golden and crispy outside, green inside.
5. Warm pita breads.
6. Fill pita with falafel, lettuce, tomato, pickles, and tahini drizzle.

---

## Hummus Platter

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/hummus-platter

Silky smooth chickpea-tahini dip with olive oil, served with warm pita and crunchy veggies — a perfect sharing plate.

**Cuisine:** Mediterranean · **Style:** Light · **Protein:** Lentils
**Time:** 10 min total (10 prep + 0 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 1/5
**Meal:** snacks · **Dietary:** Vegan · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 1 can chickpeas, drained
- 3 tbsp tahini
- 2 cloves garlic
- 3 tbsp lemon juice
- 2 tbsp olive oil
- 1/2 tsp cumin
- Paprika for garnish
- Pita bread
- Carrot and cucumber sticks
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Blend chickpeas, tahini, garlic, lemon juice, and salt until very smooth.
2. Add ice water a tablespoon at a time for desired creaminess.
3. Spread on a plate, make a well in the center.
4. Drizzle olive oil, sprinkle paprika and cumin.
5. Serve with warm pita triangles and vegetable sticks.

**Tips:**
- Peeling chickpeas before blending gives the smoothest texture.

---

## Khaman Dhokla (ખમણ ઢોકળા)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/khaman-dhokla

Soft, spongy steamed savoury cakes made from gram flour, tempered with mustard seeds, curry leaves and green chillies — a quintessential Gujarati snack.

**Cuisine:** Gujarati (Gujarat) · **Style:** Sweet · **Protein:** Lentils
**Time:** 35 min total (15 prep + 20 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 1/5
**Meal:** breakfast, snacks · **Dietary:** Vegan · Gluten-free · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 1.5 cups besan (gram flour)
- 1 tbsp semolina
- 1 tsp ginger-green chilli paste
- 1 tbsp lemon juice
- 1 tsp sugar
- 1 tsp Eno fruit salt
- 3/4 cup water
- 2 tbsp oil
- 1 tsp mustard seeds
- 1 tsp sesame seeds
- 8-10 curry leaves
- 2 green chillies, slit
- 2 tbsp sugar + 1/2 cup water for tempering
- Fresh coriander and grated coconut to garnish
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Whisk besan, semolina, ginger-chilli paste, lemon juice, sugar, salt, and water into a smooth batter.
2. Grease a steaming plate and heat water in a steamer.
3. Add Eno to the batter, whisk briskly until frothy, and pour immediately into the plate.
4. Steam on medium heat for 15-18 minutes until a toothpick comes out clean.
5. Cool slightly and cut into squares.
6. Heat oil, splutter mustard seeds, sesame seeds, curry leaves, and green chillies.
7. Add sugar and water to the tempering, bring to a quick boil, and pour evenly over the dhokla.
8. Garnish with coriander and coconut before serving.

**Tips:**
- Add Eno only when ready to steam — do not let the batter sit.
- The sugary tempering water keeps the dhokla moist and spongy.

---

## Undhiyu (ઊંધિયું)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/undhiyu

A rich winter-special mixed vegetable casserole from Surat, cooked with surti papdi, yam, baby potatoes, and spiced methi muthia in a fragrant masala.

**Cuisine:** Gujarati (Gujarat) · **Style:** One Pot · **Protein:** Veggies
**Time:** 90 min total (40 prep + 50 cook) · **Difficulty:** hard · **Servings:** 6 · **Spice:** 3/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Vegan · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 250g surti papdi (flat beans)
- 200g purple yam, cubed
- 8 baby potatoes
- 2 small brinjals
- 2 raw bananas, cubed
- 1 cup fresh methi leaves
- 1/2 cup besan
- 2 tbsp whole wheat flour
- 1 cup grated coconut
- 1/2 cup coriander leaves
- 2 tbsp ginger-green chilli paste
- 2 tsp sugar
- 1 tsp turmeric
- 2 tsp coriander-cumin powder
- 1 tsp garam masala
- 4 tbsp oil
- 1 tsp ajwain
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Mix methi, besan, wheat flour, a pinch of turmeric, salt, sugar, and ajwain. Shape into muthia dumplings and shallow fry until golden.
2. Combine grated coconut, coriander, ginger-chilli paste, coriander-cumin powder, sugar, and salt to make a stuffing masala.
3. Slit brinjals and baby potatoes and stuff with the masala.
4. Heat oil in a heavy pot, add ajwain, then layer papdi, yam, bananas, stuffed potatoes, brinjals, and muthias.
5. Sprinkle remaining masala, turmeric, garam masala, and a splash of water.
6. Cover tightly and cook on very low heat for 35-40 minutes, stirring gently once.
7. Finish with a drizzle of oil and fresh coriander.

**Tips:**
- Traditional Undhiyu is cooked upside down in a matlu — a heavy lidded pot mimics this best.
- Do not stir too often; let the vegetables steam together.

---

## Gujarati Kadhi (ગુજરાતી કઢી)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/gujarati-kadhi

Silky yogurt-and-besan soup tempered with ghee, cloves, and curry leaves, balanced with a gentle hit of sweetness — the soul of a Gujarati thali.

**Cuisine:** Gujarati (Gujarat) · **Style:** Curry · **Protein:** Lentils
**Time:** 30 min total (10 prep + 20 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 1/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Vegetarian · Gluten-free

**Ingredients:**
- 1.5 cups sour yogurt
- 3 tbsp besan
- 3 cups water
- 1 tbsp jaggery or sugar
- 1 tsp ginger-green chilli paste
- 2 tbsp ghee
- 1 tsp mustard seeds
- 1 tsp cumin seeds
- 1/2 tsp fenugreek seeds
- 3 cloves
- 1 small cinnamon stick
- 2 dried red chillies
- 10 curry leaves
- A pinch of asafoetida
- Fresh coriander to garnish
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Whisk yogurt, besan, and water into a smooth lump-free mixture.
2. Add jaggery, ginger-chilli paste, and salt. Bring to a gentle boil on medium heat, stirring constantly.
3. Simmer for 10-12 minutes until slightly thickened and the raw besan taste is gone.
4. In a small pan, heat ghee and splutter mustard seeds, cumin, fenugreek, cloves, cinnamon, red chillies, curry leaves, and asafoetida.
5. Pour the tempering over the kadhi and cover immediately for 2 minutes.
6. Garnish with coriander and serve hot with khichdi or steamed rice.

**Tips:**
- Stir continuously while heating to prevent yogurt from splitting.
- Gujarati kadhi is thinner and sweeter than Punjabi kadhi — do not skip the jaggery.

---

## Methi Thepla (મેથી થેપલા)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/methi-thepla

Soft, spiced flatbreads kneaded with fresh fenugreek leaves, yogurt, and gentle spices — a beloved travel food from Gujarati kitchens.

**Cuisine:** Gujarati (Gujarat) · **Style:** Bread Meal · **Protein:** Veggies
**Time:** 35 min total (15 prep + 20 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 2/5
**Meal:** breakfast, lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Vegetarian

**Ingredients:**
- 2 cups whole wheat flour
- 1/4 cup besan
- 1 cup chopped methi leaves
- 1/4 cup yogurt
- 1 tsp ginger-green chilli paste
- 1/2 tsp turmeric
- 1 tsp red chilli powder
- 1 tsp sesame seeds
- 1 tsp ajwain
- 2 tbsp oil + more for cooking
- Salt to taste
- Water as needed

**Steps:**
1. In a large bowl, combine flour, besan, methi, yogurt, and all spices.
2. Drizzle oil and mix, then add water little by little to form a soft, pliable dough.
3. Rest the dough for 15 minutes.
4. Divide into small balls and roll out thin discs, dusting with flour as needed.
5. Cook on a hot tawa, flipping once, and drizzle a little oil on each side until golden brown spots appear.
6. Stack and serve warm with yogurt, chhundo, or pickle.

**Tips:**
- A splash of yogurt keeps theplas soft for hours — perfect for travel.
- For extra flavour, toast the theplas with a thin smear of ghee.

---

## Khandvi (ખાંડવી)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/khandvi

Delicate gram-flour rolls cooked with buttermilk and tempered with mustard seeds — a fragrant, melt-in-mouth Gujarati teatime treat.

**Cuisine:** Gujarati (Gujarat) · **Style:** Light · **Protein:** Lentils
**Time:** 30 min total (10 prep + 20 cook) · **Difficulty:** hard · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 1/5
**Meal:** snacks · **Dietary:** Vegetarian · Gluten-free

**Ingredients:**
- 1 cup besan
- 1 cup yogurt
- 2 cups water
- 1 tsp ginger-green chilli paste
- 1/2 tsp turmeric
- 2 tbsp oil
- 1 tsp mustard seeds
- 1 tsp sesame seeds
- 2 tbsp grated coconut
- A pinch of asafoetida
- 8-10 curry leaves
- Fresh coriander to garnish
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Whisk besan, yogurt, water, ginger-chilli paste, turmeric, and salt until completely lump-free.
2. Cook on medium heat, stirring constantly, for 8-10 minutes until thick and glossy.
3. Test by spreading a spoonful on the back of a plate — if it sets firm in 1 minute, it is ready.
4. Quickly spread thin layers on the back of greased plates while hot.
5. Once cool, cut into strips and roll up tightly.
6. Heat oil, splutter mustard seeds, sesame seeds, curry leaves, and asafoetida. Pour over the rolls.
7. Garnish with coconut and coriander and serve.

**Tips:**
- Speed matters — work fast while the batter is hot or it will crack.
- Cook the batter long enough to lose the raw besan taste, otherwise the texture turns grainy.

---

## Handvo (હાંડવો)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/handvo

A savoury, oven-baked lentil and rice cake loaded with bottle gourd, carrots, and a crunchy sesame crust — hearty Gujarati comfort food.

**Cuisine:** Gujarati (Gujarat) · **Style:** Savory · **Protein:** Lentils
**Time:** 60 min total (20 prep + 40 cook) · **Difficulty:** medium · **Servings:** 6 · **Spice:** 2/5
**Meal:** breakfast, snacks · **Dietary:** Vegetarian · Gluten-free

**Ingredients:**
- 1 cup rice
- 1/4 cup toor dal
- 1/4 cup chana dal
- 2 tbsp urad dal
- 1 cup yogurt
- 1 cup grated bottle gourd
- 1/2 cup grated carrot
- 1 tsp ginger-green chilli paste
- 1/2 tsp turmeric
- 1 tsp red chilli powder
- 1 tsp Eno fruit salt
- 3 tbsp oil
- 1 tsp mustard seeds
- 1 tbsp sesame seeds
- 1 tsp cumin seeds
- 10 curry leaves
- A pinch of asafoetida
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Soak rice and dals together for 4 hours, drain, and grind with yogurt into a coarse batter.
2. Ferment overnight (or 6-8 hours) in a warm place.
3. Stir in grated vegetables, ginger-chilli paste, turmeric, chilli powder, and salt.
4. Just before baking, add Eno and mix gently.
5. Heat oil in an oven-safe pan, splutter mustard seeds, cumin, sesame seeds, curry leaves, and asafoetida.
6. Pour the batter over the tempering, sprinkle more sesame seeds on top, cover and cook on low for 15 minutes.
7. Finish in a 180°C oven for 20-25 minutes until the top is deeply golden and crusty.
8. Cool 5 minutes, slice, and serve with green chutney.

**Tips:**
- Fermentation gives Handvo its characteristic tang — do not rush it.
- A cast-iron pan gives the best crunchy bottom crust.

---

## Gujarati Toor Dal (ગુજરાતી દાળ)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/gujarati-dal

A signature sweet-sour-spicy toor dal with jaggery, kokum, peanuts, and a ghee tempering — the gentle backbone of a Gujarati meal.

**Cuisine:** Gujarati (Gujarat) · **Style:** Curry · **Protein:** Lentils
**Time:** 35 min total (10 prep + 25 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 2/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Vegetarian · Gluten-free

**Ingredients:**
- 1 cup toor dal
- 4 cups water
- 2 tbsp raw peanuts
- 1 tbsp jaggery
- 1 tbsp lemon juice or 2 kokum petals
- 1 tomato, chopped
- 1 tsp ginger-green chilli paste
- 1/2 tsp turmeric
- 1 tsp coriander-cumin powder
- 2 tbsp ghee
- 1 tsp mustard seeds
- 1 tsp cumin seeds
- 2 cloves
- 1 small cinnamon stick
- 1 dried red chilli
- 8 curry leaves
- A pinch of asafoetida
- Fresh coriander to garnish
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Pressure cook toor dal with peanuts, turmeric, and water for 3-4 whistles until very soft.
2. Whisk the cooked dal smooth and add tomato, jaggery, kokum, ginger-chilli paste, coriander-cumin powder, and salt.
3. Simmer for 10-12 minutes until well blended.
4. Heat ghee in a small pan and splutter mustard seeds, cumin, cloves, cinnamon, red chilli, curry leaves, and asafoetida.
5. Pour the tempering over the dal.
6. Finish with lemon juice (if not using kokum) and coriander.
7. Serve with steamed rice and a side of sabzi.

**Tips:**
- The balance of sweet, sour, and spicy is the defining trait — taste and adjust before serving.
- Soaked peanuts add a lovely bite and richness.

---

## Fafda (ફાફડા)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/fafda-jalebi

Crisp, ribbon-shaped gram flour fritters seasoned with ajwain and black pepper — the weekend breakfast icon of Gujarat, usually paired with jalebi.

**Cuisine:** Gujarati (Gujarat) · **Style:** Fried · **Protein:** Lentils
**Time:** 40 min total (20 prep + 20 cook) · **Difficulty:** medium · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 2/5
**Meal:** breakfast, snacks · **Dietary:** Vegan · Gluten-free · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 2 cups besan
- 2 tbsp oil
- 1/2 tsp ajwain
- 1/2 tsp black pepper powder
- 1/4 tsp baking soda
- 1/2 tsp papad khar (optional)
- Water as needed
- Oil for deep frying
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Mix besan with salt, ajwain, pepper, baking soda, and papad khar.
2. Add 2 tbsp oil and knead with warm water into a stiff, smooth dough.
3. Rest the dough for 15 minutes.
4. Take a small portion and press it flat on a greased board into a long ribbon using the heel of your palm.
5. Lift carefully with a knife and slide into hot oil on medium-low heat.
6. Fry until crisp but not too dark — about 2 minutes per fafda.
7. Drain on paper and serve warm with papaya sambharo, green chutney, and fried chillies.

**Tips:**
- Papad khar gives the characteristic crunch — skip it and the fafda turns hard.
- Keep the oil on medium — too hot and they colour before cooking through.

---

## Sev Tameta nu Shaak (સેવ ટમેટા નું શાક)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/sev-tameta-nu-shaak

A tangy, quick tomato curry topped with crunchy sev — a beloved everyday Gujarati sabzi that comes together in minutes.

**Cuisine:** Gujarati (Gujarat) · **Style:** Curry · **Protein:** Veggies
**Time:** 25 min total (10 prep + 15 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 2/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Vegetarian · Gluten-free

**Ingredients:**
- 5 ripe tomatoes, pureed
- 2 tbsp oil
- 1 tsp mustard seeds
- 1 tsp cumin seeds
- A pinch of asafoetida
- 8 curry leaves
- 1 tsp ginger-green chilli paste
- 1/2 tsp turmeric
- 1 tsp red chilli powder
- 1 tsp coriander-cumin powder
- 1 tbsp jaggery
- 1 cup thick sev (nylon sev)
- Fresh coriander to garnish
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Heat oil and splutter mustard seeds, cumin, curry leaves, and asafoetida.
2. Add ginger-chilli paste and sauté for 30 seconds.
3. Pour in tomato puree, turmeric, chilli powder, coriander-cumin powder, jaggery, and salt.
4. Simmer for 8-10 minutes until oil separates.
5. Adjust consistency with a splash of water if needed.
6. Just before serving, stir in the sev so it stays slightly crunchy.
7. Garnish with fresh coriander and serve hot with rotli or bhakhri.

**Tips:**
- Add sev only at the last moment, otherwise it turns soggy.
- Use ripe, sour tomatoes — the tang is what makes this dish.

---

## Misal Pav (मिसळ पाव)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/misal-pav

A fiery Pune-born sprouted moth bean curry topped with crunchy farsan, onions, and coriander — served with soft pav for scooping.

**Cuisine:** Maharashtrian (Maharashtra) · **Style:** Curry · **Protein:** Lentils
**Time:** 60 min total (20 prep + 40 cook) · **Difficulty:** medium · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 4/5
**Meal:** breakfast, lunch, snacks · **Dietary:** Vegan · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 2 cups sprouted moth beans
- 2 large onions, chopped
- 3 tomatoes, pureed
- 2 tbsp goda masala
- 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
- 1 tsp turmeric
- 2 tsp red chilli powder
- 3 tbsp oil
- 1 tsp mustard seeds
- 1 tsp cumin seeds
- 10 curry leaves
- 1 cup farsan or mixture
- 1 lemon, wedged
- Fresh coriander, chopped
- 8 pav buns
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Pressure cook sprouted moth beans with salt and turmeric until just soft.
2. Heat oil, splutter mustard and cumin seeds, curry leaves.
3. Add onions and sauté until deep golden.
4. Stir in ginger-garlic paste, then tomato puree, goda masala, and red chilli powder.
5. Cook until oil separates, then add cooked beans and 2 cups of water.
6. Simmer for 15 minutes to let the masala soak in.
7. Ladle into bowls, top with farsan, raw onions, coriander, and a squeeze of lemon.
8. Serve with toasted buttered pav.

**Tips:**
- Use homemade goda masala if you can — it gives the signature smoky-sweet depth.
- Add farsan only at the table so it stays crunchy.

---

## Vada Pav (वडा पाव)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/vada-pav

Mumbai's beloved potato fritter sandwiched in a buttered pav with garlic chutney and green chilli — the original Indian burger.

**Cuisine:** Maharashtrian (Maharashtra) · **Style:** Fried · **Protein:** Veggies
**Time:** 40 min total (20 prep + 20 cook) · **Difficulty:** medium · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 3/5
**Meal:** snacks · **Dietary:** Vegetarian

**Ingredients:**
- 4 large potatoes, boiled and mashed
- 1 tbsp ginger-garlic-green chilli paste
- 1 tsp mustard seeds
- 8 curry leaves
- 1/2 tsp turmeric
- 1/4 tsp asafoetida
- 1 cup besan
- 1/4 tsp baking soda
- 8 pav buns
- 2 tbsp butter
- Dry garlic chutney
- Fried green chillies
- Oil for deep frying
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Temper mustard seeds, curry leaves, turmeric, and asafoetida in oil.
2. Add ginger-garlic-chilli paste and sauté, then fold into mashed potatoes with salt.
3. Shape into smooth balls and rest for 10 minutes.
4. Make a thick batter from besan, baking soda, salt, and water.
5. Dip each vada in the batter and deep fry until crisp and golden.
6. Slit pav, toast with butter, smear with garlic chutney.
7. Place a vada inside, top with a fried chilli, and serve hot.

**Tips:**
- Keep the batter lump-free and thick enough to coat evenly.
- Dry garlic chutney is non-negotiable — it defines vada pav.

---

## Puran Poli (पुरण पोळी)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/puran-poli

A festive sweet flatbread stuffed with sweetened chana dal and cardamom, cooked on a griddle with ghee — Maharashtrian celebration food.

**Cuisine:** Maharashtrian (Maharashtra) · **Style:** Sweet · **Protein:** Lentils
**Time:** 60 min total (30 prep + 30 cook) · **Difficulty:** hard · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 1/5
**Meal:** breakfast, snacks · **Dietary:** Vegetarian

**Ingredients:**
- 1 cup chana dal
- 1 cup jaggery, grated
- 1 tsp cardamom powder
- 1/4 tsp nutmeg powder
- 1.5 cups whole wheat flour
- 1/4 cup fine semolina
- 3 tbsp oil
- Ghee for cooking
- A pinch of salt
- Water as needed

**Steps:**
1. Cook chana dal until soft but not mushy; drain very well.
2. On low heat, mash dal with jaggery, cardamom, and nutmeg until a thick puran forms. Cool.
3. Knead flour, semolina, oil, salt, and water into a soft, rested dough.
4. Take a dough ball, flatten, place a larger ball of puran inside, seal, and roll gently into a disc.
5. Cook on a hot tawa, flipping with generous ghee until golden on both sides.
6. Serve warm with extra ghee, milk, or saar (thin dal).

**Tips:**
- Drain dal thoroughly — excess water ruins the puran consistency.
- Roll gently; too much pressure will tear the dough.

---

## Bharli Vangi (भरली वांगी)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/bharli-vangi

Baby brinjals stuffed with a peanut-coconut-jaggery masala and slow-cooked — a signature Maharashtrian sabzi with a sweet-sour-nutty profile.

**Cuisine:** Maharashtrian (Maharashtra) · **Style:** Curry · **Protein:** Veggies
**Time:** 50 min total (20 prep + 30 cook) · **Difficulty:** medium · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 3/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Vegan · Gluten-free · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 8 small baby brinjals
- 1/2 cup roasted peanuts, powdered
- 1/4 cup dry coconut, grated
- 2 tbsp goda masala
- 1 tbsp jaggery
- 1 tbsp tamarind paste
- 1 tsp ginger-garlic paste
- 2 tsp red chilli powder
- 1 tsp turmeric
- 3 tbsp oil
- 1 tsp mustard seeds
- 1 tsp cumin seeds
- 1/4 tsp asafoetida
- Fresh coriander to garnish
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Mix powdered peanuts, coconut, goda masala, red chilli, turmeric, jaggery, tamarind, ginger-garlic paste, and salt.
2. Slit brinjals crosswise keeping the stem intact; stuff with the masala.
3. Heat oil, splutter mustard and cumin seeds, and asafoetida.
4. Place stuffed brinjals cut-side down and sear for 2 minutes.
5. Add any leftover masala with 1 cup of water, cover and cook 20-25 minutes on low.
6. Baste brinjals occasionally until tender and glossy.
7. Garnish with coriander and serve with jowar bhakri or rice.

**Tips:**
- Choose small, tender brinjals — they cook through evenly.
- Don't skip tamarind and jaggery together — they give the classic Maharashtrian balance.

---

## Fish Moilee (മീൻ മോയിലി)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/fish-moilee-kerala

A gentle Kerala fish stew in a creamy coconut milk gravy with ginger, green chillies, and curry leaves — delicate, coastal, and deeply fragrant.

**Cuisine:** Kerala (Kerala) · **Style:** Curry · **Protein:** Fish
**Time:** 40 min total (15 prep + 25 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 2/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Non-Veg · Gluten-free · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 500g firm white fish (seer/kingfish)
- 1 cup thick coconut milk
- 1 cup thin coconut milk
- 2 onions, sliced
- 4 green chillies, slit
- 2 tbsp ginger, julienned
- 1 tomato, sliced
- 1/2 tsp turmeric
- 15 curry leaves
- 3 tbsp coconut oil
- 1/2 tsp fenugreek seeds
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Marinate fish with turmeric and salt for 10 minutes.
2. Heat coconut oil, temper fenugreek seeds and curry leaves.
3. Sauté ginger, green chillies, and onions until soft but not browned.
4. Add thin coconut milk, turmeric, and salt. Bring to a gentle simmer.
5. Slide in the fish pieces and tomato. Cook 8-10 minutes.
6. Stir in thick coconut milk and warm through on very low heat — do not boil.
7. Finish with extra curry leaves and serve with appam or steamed rice.

**Tips:**
- Never boil after adding thick coconut milk — it will split.
- Coconut oil is essential for the authentic flavour.

---

## Puttu (പുട്ട്)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/puttu

Steamed cylinders of rice flour layered with fresh coconut — a classic Kerala breakfast served with kadala curry or ripe bananas and sugar.

**Cuisine:** Kerala (Kerala) · **Style:** Light · **Protein:** Veggies
**Time:** 25 min total (10 prep + 15 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 3 · **Spice:** 1/5
**Meal:** breakfast · **Dietary:** Vegan · Gluten-free · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 2 cups puttu rice flour (roasted)
- 1 cup fresh grated coconut
- 3/4 cup warm water
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Mix puttu flour with salt. Drizzle warm water and rub between palms until it resembles breadcrumbs.
2. Test: a fistful should just hold together, then crumble when tapped.
3. Layer the puttu maker: coconut, flour, coconut, flour — ending with coconut on top.
4. Steam for 8-10 minutes until fragrant.
5. Gently push out the cylinder and serve hot with kadala curry, banana, or papadam.

**Tips:**
- Too much water makes puttu dense — go slow.
- Pre-roasted puttu flour gives the best texture.

---

## Cabbage Thoran (തോരൻ)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/thoran

Finely shredded cabbage stir-fried with freshly ground coconut, green chilli, and curry leaves — a dry, aromatic Kerala side dish.

**Cuisine:** Kerala (Kerala) · **Style:** Dry · **Protein:** Veggies
**Time:** 25 min total (10 prep + 15 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 2/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Vegan · Gluten-free · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 1/2 medium cabbage, finely shredded
- 1 cup fresh grated coconut
- 2 green chillies
- 4 small shallots
- 1/2 tsp cumin seeds
- 1/2 tsp turmeric
- 2 tbsp coconut oil
- 1 tsp mustard seeds
- 2 dried red chillies
- 12 curry leaves
- 1 tbsp urad dal
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Coarsely pound coconut with green chillies, shallots, cumin, and turmeric.
2. Heat coconut oil, splutter mustard seeds, urad dal, red chillies, and curry leaves.
3. Add cabbage and salt, toss and cover for 3-4 minutes until just softened.
4. Uncover, stir in the coconut mix, and cook on high for 3 minutes — do not let cabbage brown.
5. Serve hot as part of a sadya or with rice and rasam.

**Tips:**
- Keep the heat high and the cook time short — thoran should stay bright and crunchy.
- Freshly grated coconut is non-negotiable.

---

## Nadan Kozhi Curry (നാടൻ കോഴി കറി)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/nadan-kozhi-curry

A robust Kerala country-style chicken curry simmered in roasted coconut, pepper, and whole spices — dark, fragrant, and deeply warming.

**Cuisine:** Kerala (Kerala) · **Style:** Curry · **Protein:** Chicken
**Time:** 60 min total (20 prep + 40 cook) · **Difficulty:** medium · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 3/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Non-Veg · Gluten-free · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 750g chicken, bone-in pieces
- 1/2 cup fresh coconut, grated
- 2 tbsp coriander seeds
- 1 tsp fennel seeds
- 1 tsp black peppercorns
- 3 dried red chillies
- 2 large onions, sliced
- 2 tomatoes, chopped
- 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
- 1 tsp turmeric
- 3 tbsp coconut oil
- 15 curry leaves
- 1 tsp garam masala
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Dry roast coconut with coriander, fennel, peppercorns, and red chillies until deep brown. Cool and grind to a paste.
2. Heat coconut oil, sauté curry leaves and onions until deep golden.
3. Add ginger-garlic paste, turmeric, and tomatoes. Cook until soft.
4. Add chicken, salt, and sear on high for 5 minutes.
5. Stir in the roasted coconut paste and 2 cups of water.
6. Simmer covered for 25-30 minutes until chicken is tender and gravy is thick.
7. Finish with garam masala and more curry leaves. Serve with appam or parotta.

**Tips:**
- Roasting the coconut till dark is what gives the curry its depth — don't shortcut this.
- Freshly ground pepper lifts the whole dish.

---

## Chicken Vindaloo (विंदालू)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/chicken-vindaloo

A fiery Goan-Portuguese chicken curry in a vinegar-and-red-chilli masala — sharp, spicy, and unmistakably coastal.

**Cuisine:** Goan (Goa) · **Style:** Curry · **Protein:** Chicken
**Time:** 65 min total (25 prep + 40 cook) · **Difficulty:** medium · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 4/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Non-Veg · Gluten-free · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 750g chicken on the bone
- 10 Kashmiri dried red chillies
- 1 tbsp cumin seeds
- 1 tsp black peppercorns
- 1 tsp cloves
- 1 small cinnamon stick
- 8 garlic cloves
- 1 inch ginger
- 3 tbsp palm/cider vinegar
- 1 tsp sugar
- 2 large onions, sliced
- 3 tbsp oil
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Soak Kashmiri chillies in warm water for 20 minutes.
2. Grind chillies, cumin, pepper, cloves, cinnamon, garlic, ginger, and vinegar into a smooth vindaloo paste.
3. Marinate chicken with half the paste, sugar, and salt for at least 2 hours.
4. Heat oil, sauté onions until golden brown.
5. Add remaining vindaloo paste and cook until oil separates.
6. Add marinated chicken, sear well, then add 1 cup of water.
7. Simmer covered for 25-30 minutes until chicken is tender.
8. Adjust vinegar and salt. Rest overnight for the best flavour. Serve with pao or rice.

**Tips:**
- Vindaloo only gets better the next day — make it ahead.
- Palm vinegar gives the authentic Goan tang.

---

## Prawn Balchão (बाल्चाव)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/prawn-balchao

A pickle-like Goan prawn preparation bursting with vinegar, dried red chillies, and spices — intense, bright, and best served in small spoonfuls.

**Cuisine:** Goan (Goa) · **Style:** Dry · **Protein:** Prawn
**Time:** 45 min total (20 prep + 25 cook) · **Difficulty:** medium · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 4/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Non-Veg · Gluten-free · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 500g small prawns, cleaned
- 12 Kashmiri dried red chillies
- 1 tsp cumin seeds
- 1 tsp peppercorns
- 6 cloves
- 1 tsp turmeric
- 10 garlic cloves
- 1 inch ginger
- 1/4 cup palm vinegar
- 2 large onions, finely chopped
- 2 tomatoes, chopped
- 1 tbsp sugar
- 4 tbsp oil
- 1 tsp mustard seeds
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Grind red chillies, cumin, peppercorns, cloves, turmeric, garlic, ginger, and vinegar to a paste.
2. Pat prawns dry and lightly sauté in oil for 2 minutes. Set aside.
3. In the same oil, splutter mustard seeds, then sauté onions until deep brown.
4. Add the spice paste and cook on low until oil separates.
5. Stir in tomatoes and sugar. Cook until jammy.
6. Add prawns and cook 5-6 minutes until coated and glossy.
7. Cool completely and store — it tastes even better after a day.

**Tips:**
- Balchão is a preserve — use enough vinegar and cook until completely dry.
- Serve small portions with rice or bread; it is intensely flavoured.

---

## Chicken Xacuti (शाकूती)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/chicken-xacuti

A complex Goan chicken curry in a roasted coconut-and-whole-spice masala with star anise, nutmeg, and poppy seeds — deep, nutty, and warming.

**Cuisine:** Goan (Goa) · **Style:** Curry · **Protein:** Chicken
**Time:** 70 min total (25 prep + 45 cook) · **Difficulty:** hard · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 3/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Non-Veg · Gluten-free · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 750g chicken, bone-in pieces
- 1 cup fresh coconut, grated
- 1 tbsp coriander seeds
- 1 tsp cumin seeds
- 1 tsp fennel seeds
- 8 cloves
- 1 cinnamon stick
- 2 star anise
- 1/2 nutmeg, grated
- 1 tbsp poppy seeds
- 6 dried red chillies
- 2 large onions, sliced
- 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
- 1 tsp turmeric
- 3 tbsp oil
- 2 tbsp tamarind paste
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Dry roast coconut with all whole spices, red chillies, and poppy seeds until fragrant and golden.
2. Cool and grind with a little water to a fine paste.
3. Heat oil and sauté onions until deep brown. Add ginger-garlic paste.
4. Add chicken, turmeric, and salt. Sear for 5 minutes.
5. Stir in the xacuti paste and 2 cups of water.
6. Cover and simmer for 25-30 minutes until chicken is tender.
7. Add tamarind, taste, and adjust. Serve with rice or poee bread.

**Tips:**
- Roast each whole spice individually for maximum aroma — the masala carries this dish.
- Xacuti tastes even better the next day.

---

## Dal Baati Churma (दाल बाटी चूरमा)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/dal-baati-churma

Rajasthan's legendary trio — hard-baked wheat dumplings served with five-lentil dal and a sweet crumbled wheat pudding drenched in ghee.

**Cuisine:** Rajasthani (Rajasthan) · **Style:** Bread Meal · **Protein:** Lentils
**Time:** 90 min total (30 prep + 60 cook) · **Difficulty:** hard · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 2/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Vegetarian

**Ingredients:**
- 2 cups whole wheat flour (for baati)
- 1/4 cup semolina
- 1/2 cup ghee + extra for soaking
- 1/4 cup yogurt
- 1/4 cup each toor, chana, moong, urad, masoor dal
- 2 tomatoes, pureed
- 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
- 2 tsp red chilli powder
- 1 tsp turmeric
- 1 tsp garam masala
- 1 tsp cumin seeds
- 1/2 cup jaggery
- 1/4 cup chopped almonds and raisins
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Knead wheat flour, semolina, yogurt, melted ghee, and salt into a stiff dough. Rest 20 minutes.
2. Shape into balls and bake at 200°C for 30-35 minutes until cracked and golden.
3. For the dal: pressure cook all five dals with salt and turmeric.
4. Temper cumin, garlic, and tomatoes in ghee; stir in dal, chilli, and garam masala. Simmer 10 minutes.
5. For churma: coarsely crush two baatis, mix with jaggery, ghee, cardamom, almonds, and raisins.
6. Soak hot baatis briefly in ghee before serving.
7. Serve baati with dal poured over and churma on the side.

**Tips:**
- The baati must sound hollow when tapped — that means it's cooked through.
- Generous ghee is not optional here, it's the whole point.

---

## Gatte ki Sabzi (गट्टे की सब्ज़ी)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/gatte-ki-sabzi

Spiced besan dumplings simmered in a tangy yogurt gravy — a desert-state classic born out of necessity and now beloved everywhere.

**Cuisine:** Rajasthani (Rajasthan) · **Style:** Curry · **Protein:** Lentils
**Time:** 45 min total (15 prep + 30 cook) · **Difficulty:** medium · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 3/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Vegetarian · Gluten-free

**Ingredients:**
- 1.5 cups besan
- 1 tsp ajwain
- 1/2 tsp red chilli powder
- 1/2 tsp turmeric
- 3 tbsp oil + more for dough
- 1 cup sour yogurt
- 1 tsp coriander powder
- 1 tsp cumin seeds
- 1/2 tsp mustard seeds
- 2 dried red chillies
- 1 tbsp ginger-green chilli paste
- 1 tsp garam masala
- A pinch of asafoetida
- Fresh coriander to garnish
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Knead besan with ajwain, chilli powder, turmeric, salt, and oil into a stiff dough.
2. Roll into long finger-thick logs. Boil in salted water for 8-10 minutes until they float.
3. Cool and slice into 1 cm pieces. Reserve the cooking water.
4. Whisk yogurt with coriander powder, turmeric, chilli, and salt.
5. Heat oil, splutter cumin, mustard seeds, red chillies, and asafoetida.
6. Add ginger-chilli paste, then lower heat and slowly stir in yogurt to prevent splitting.
7. Add gatte and reserved cooking water. Simmer 10 minutes.
8. Finish with garam masala and coriander. Serve with bajra roti or rice.

**Tips:**
- Stir yogurt continuously as you add it — never dump it all at once.
- Slightly sour yogurt gives the best flavour.

---

## Laal Maas (लाल मांस)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/laal-maas

A blazing red Rajasthani mutton curry built on Mathania chillies, garlic, and smoky ghee — fierce, elegant, and famously royal.

**Cuisine:** Rajasthani (Rajasthan) · **Style:** Curry · **Protein:** Mutton
**Time:** 100 min total (20 prep + 80 cook) · **Difficulty:** hard · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 5/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Non-Veg · Gluten-free

**Ingredients:**
- 750g mutton on the bone
- 15 Mathania (or Kashmiri) dried red chillies
- 12 garlic cloves
- 3 tbsp ghee
- 2 large onions, sliced
- 1 cup yogurt
- 2 tsp coriander powder
- 1 tsp cumin powder
- 1 tsp garam masala
- 4 cloves
- 2 black cardamoms
- 1 bay leaf
- A small piece of charcoal
- 1 tsp ghee for smoking
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Soak chillies in warm water for 30 minutes, then grind with garlic and a little water to a smooth paste.
2. Heat ghee, sauté onions until deep brown. Add cloves, cardamom, and bay leaf.
3. Add mutton and sear on high for 8-10 minutes.
4. Whisk yogurt with coriander powder, cumin, and the chilli-garlic paste. Add to the pot.
5. Cook until oil separates, then add 3 cups of water and salt.
6. Pressure cook or slow-simmer for 45-60 minutes until mutton is very tender.
7. Place a small steel bowl inside the pot, put a red-hot charcoal piece in it, drizzle ghee, cover immediately for 3 minutes for dhungar smoke.
8. Finish with garam masala. Serve with bajra roti.

**Tips:**
- Mathania chillies are the traditional choice — hot but not bitter.
- The charcoal smoke step is what turns this from good to legendary.

---

## Hyderabadi Chicken Biryani (حیدرآبادی بریانی)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/hyderabadi-chicken-biryani

Layered basmati rice and saffron-kissed marinated chicken sealed and slow-cooked dum-style — the Nizam-era heirloom of Indian biryanis.

**Cuisine:** Hyderabadi (Hyderabad) · **Style:** Rice Bowl · **Protein:** Chicken
**Time:** 90 min total (40 prep + 50 cook) · **Difficulty:** hard · **Servings:** 6 · **Spice:** 3/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Non-Veg · Gluten-free

**Ingredients:**
- 750g chicken, bone-in
- 3 cups basmati rice
- 1.5 cups yogurt
- 3 large onions, thinly sliced and fried (birista)
- 2 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
- 1 tbsp green chilli paste
- 2 tsp red chilli powder
- 1 tsp turmeric
- 2 tsp biryani masala
- 1/4 cup mint leaves
- 1/4 cup coriander leaves
- 1/2 tsp saffron soaked in warm milk
- 1/4 cup ghee
- 6 green cardamoms
- 4 cloves
- 1 cinnamon stick
- 2 bay leaves
- 2 tbsp lemon juice
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Marinate chicken with yogurt, half the fried onions, ginger-garlic paste, chilli paste, red chilli, turmeric, biryani masala, salt, lemon juice, half the mint and coriander. Rest 1 hour.
2. Boil salted water with whole spices, add soaked basmati, and cook rice until 70 percent done. Drain.
3. In a heavy pot, layer marinated chicken at the bottom.
4. Top with rice, remaining fried onions, mint, coriander, saffron milk, and ghee.
5. Seal the lid with dough or foil. Cook on high for 3 minutes, then lowest heat for 30 minutes.
6. Rest sealed for 10 minutes before opening.
7. Gently mix top and bottom. Serve with mirchi ka salan and raita.

**Tips:**
- Rice must be only 70 percent cooked before layering — it finishes on dum.
- Don't peek under the lid during dum — you lose the steam that cooks everything.

---

## Mirchi ka Salan (مرچی کا سالن)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/mirchi-ka-salan

Long green chillies simmered in a creamy peanut, sesame, and tamarind gravy — Hyderabad's signature biryani accompaniment, sweet-tangy-spicy all at once.

**Cuisine:** Hyderabadi (Hyderabad) · **Style:** Curry · **Protein:** Veggies
**Time:** 45 min total (15 prep + 30 cook) · **Difficulty:** medium · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 3/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Vegan · Gluten-free · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 10-12 long green chillies (Bhavnagari), slit
- 1/2 cup roasted peanuts
- 2 tbsp sesame seeds
- 2 tbsp desiccated coconut
- 1 tbsp poppy seeds
- 1 tbsp coriander seeds
- 1 tsp cumin seeds
- 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
- 1 tsp turmeric
- 2 tbsp tamarind paste
- 3 tbsp oil
- 1 tsp mustard seeds
- 1 tsp nigella seeds
- 10 curry leaves
- 1 tsp jaggery
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Dry roast peanuts, sesame, coconut, poppy seeds, coriander, and cumin. Cool and grind to a smooth paste with water.
2. Heat oil, splutter mustard seeds, nigella seeds, and curry leaves.
3. Slide in the slit green chillies and sauté 3-4 minutes. Set aside.
4. In the same oil, add ginger-garlic paste and turmeric, then pour in the peanut-sesame paste.
5. Cook 5 minutes, then add 2 cups of water, tamarind, jaggery, and salt.
6. Return the chillies to the pan. Simmer covered 15-20 minutes until oil floats on top.
7. Rest an hour before serving with biryani or rice.

**Tips:**
- Bhavnagari chillies are mild — use them if your regular green chillies are too hot.
- The salan should taste balanced: spicy, tangy, sweet, and earthy.

---

## Haleem (حلیم)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/haleem

A Ramadan-special slow-cooked porridge of wheat, lentils, and mutton pounded to a silky, rich stew — garnished with fried onions, lemon, and mint.

**Cuisine:** Hyderabadi (Hyderabad) · **Style:** One Pot · **Protein:** Mutton
**Time:** 210 min total (30 prep + 180 cook) · **Difficulty:** hard · **Servings:** 6 · **Spice:** 3/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Non-Veg

**Ingredients:**
- 500g mutton, boneless
- 1 cup cracked wheat (dalia)
- 1/4 cup chana dal
- 1/4 cup moong dal
- 1/4 cup urad dal
- 3 large onions, sliced and fried (birista)
- 2 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
- 2 tsp red chilli powder
- 1 tsp turmeric
- 1 tbsp biryani masala
- 1/2 cup yogurt
- 4 tbsp ghee
- 1 tsp cumin seeds
- 4 green cardamoms
- 4 cloves
- 1 cinnamon stick
- Lemon wedges, mint leaves, and green chillies to serve
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Soak wheat and dals separately for 1 hour.
2. In a large pot, heat ghee. Add whole spices and cumin.
3. Add mutton, ginger-garlic paste, yogurt, chilli, turmeric, biryani masala, and salt. Sear 5 minutes.
4. Add soaked wheat and dals with 8 cups of water. Bring to a boil.
5. Cover and simmer on lowest heat for 2.5-3 hours, stirring every 20 minutes.
6. When everything collapses, use a wooden masher or hand blender to pound the mixture smooth but still textured.
7. Finish with fried onions and ghee.
8. Serve topped with more fried onions, mint, green chillies, and lemon wedges.

**Tips:**
- Haleem needs patience — long, slow cooking is what gives the silky texture.
- A brief hand-blender pulse saves your arm, but don't overdo it.

---

## Rogan Josh (روغن جوش)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/rogan-josh-kashmiri

A deep crimson Kashmiri lamb curry with Kashmiri chillies, fennel, and dried ginger — aromatic, delicate, and unmistakable.

**Cuisine:** Kashmiri (Kashmir) · **Style:** Curry · **Protein:** Mutton
**Time:** 95 min total (20 prep + 75 cook) · **Difficulty:** medium · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 2/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Non-Veg · Gluten-free

**Ingredients:**
- 750g lamb/mutton on the bone
- 3 tbsp mustard oil
- 1 tbsp ghee
- 2 black cardamoms
- 4 green cardamoms
- 6 cloves
- 1 cinnamon stick
- 2 bay leaves
- 2 tbsp Kashmiri red chilli powder
- 2 tsp fennel powder
- 1 tsp dry ginger powder (sonth)
- 1/2 cup yogurt
- A pinch of asafoetida
- A pinch of saffron
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Heat mustard oil until smoking, then lower and add ghee.
2. Splutter whole spices and asafoetida.
3. Add mutton and sear on high for 8 minutes until deep brown.
4. Whisk yogurt with Kashmiri chilli, fennel powder, dry ginger, and salt. Add slowly, stirring.
5. Cook on medium until yogurt is absorbed and oil separates.
6. Add 3 cups of water and bring to a boil.
7. Cover and simmer on low for 60-70 minutes until mutton is falling-off-bone tender.
8. Finish with a pinch of saffron. Serve with steamed rice or naan.

**Tips:**
- No onion, no garlic, no tomatoes — authentic Rogan Josh gets its colour from Kashmiri chilli alone.
- Mustard oil tempered properly is essential for flavour.

---

## Mutton Yakhni (یخنی)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/kashmiri-yakhni

A white Kashmiri mutton curry in a gentle yogurt, fennel, and cardamom gravy — subtle, fragrant, and soul-warming.

**Cuisine:** Kashmiri (Kashmir) · **Style:** Curry · **Protein:** Mutton
**Time:** 85 min total (15 prep + 70 cook) · **Difficulty:** medium · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 1/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Non-Veg · Gluten-free

**Ingredients:**
- 750g mutton on the bone
- 2 cups yogurt, whisked
- 2 tbsp ghee
- 4 green cardamoms
- 2 black cardamoms
- 4 cloves
- 1 cinnamon stick
- 1 tbsp fennel powder
- 1 tsp dry ginger powder
- 1/2 tsp white pepper
- A pinch of asafoetida
- A few strands of saffron
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Boil mutton with salt, 1 cardamom, 1 clove, and enough water until tender. Reserve stock.
2. Heat ghee in a clean pot. Temper remaining cardamoms, cloves, cinnamon, and asafoetida.
3. Add cooked mutton and sear 2 minutes.
4. Lower heat completely. Slowly pour in whisked yogurt while stirring constantly.
5. Add fennel powder, dry ginger, white pepper, and reserved stock.
6. Simmer very gently for 20 minutes until thick and creamy — never let it boil hard.
7. Finish with saffron. Serve with Kashmiri rice.

**Tips:**
- Yogurt must go in slowly and at low heat, or it will split.
- Yakhni is pale on purpose — don't be tempted to add turmeric or chilli.

---

## Dum Aloo Kashmiri (दम आलू)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/dum-aloo-kashmiri

Baby potatoes pricked and simmered in a velvety yogurt, fennel, and Kashmiri chilli gravy — vegetarian but unmistakably Kashmiri.

**Cuisine:** Kashmiri (Kashmir) · **Style:** Curry · **Protein:** Veggies
**Time:** 50 min total (15 prep + 35 cook) · **Difficulty:** medium · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 2/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Vegetarian · Gluten-free

**Ingredients:**
- 500g baby potatoes
- 1 cup yogurt, whisked
- 2 tbsp mustard oil
- 1 tbsp ghee
- 3 green cardamoms
- 1 black cardamom
- 4 cloves
- 1 cinnamon stick
- 2 tsp Kashmiri red chilli powder
- 1 tbsp fennel powder
- 1 tsp dry ginger powder
- A pinch of asafoetida
- A pinch of saffron
- Salt to taste
- Oil for frying potatoes

**Steps:**
1. Peel boiled baby potatoes and prick them all over with a toothpick.
2. Fry potatoes in hot oil until golden. Set aside.
3. Heat mustard oil and ghee. Temper whole spices and asafoetida.
4. Lower heat, whisk yogurt with Kashmiri chilli, fennel, dry ginger, and salt, then stir into the oil slowly.
5. Cook gently until oil separates — do not let yogurt split.
6. Add fried potatoes and 1.5 cups of water.
7. Cover and simmer 15-20 minutes until gravy is thick and clings to the potatoes.
8. Finish with saffron. Serve with rice or naan.

**Tips:**
- Pricking potatoes lets them soak up the gravy — don't skip it.
- Kashmiri red chilli is about colour, not heat.

---

## Pad Thai (ผัดไทย)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/pad-thai-prawn

Wok-tossed rice noodles with prawns, tofu, egg, and crushed peanuts in a tamarind-fish-sauce glaze — Thailand's most iconic street food.

**Cuisine:** Thai (Thailand) · **Style:** One Pot · **Protein:** Prawn
**Time:** 30 min total (20 prep + 10 cook) · **Difficulty:** medium · **Servings:** 2 · **Spice:** 2/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Non-Veg · Contains egg · Gluten-free · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 200g flat rice noodles, soaked
- 200g prawns, peeled
- 100g firm tofu, cubed
- 2 eggs, lightly beaten
- 3 tbsp tamarind paste
- 2 tbsp fish sauce
- 2 tbsp palm sugar
- 1 tsp chilli flakes
- 3 garlic cloves, minced
- 1/2 cup bean sprouts
- 3 spring onions, cut into 2-inch pieces
- 3 tbsp oil
- 1/4 cup roasted peanuts, crushed
- Lime wedges to serve

**Steps:**
1. Whisk tamarind, fish sauce, palm sugar, and chilli flakes into a pad thai sauce.
2. Heat oil in a wok until very hot. Add garlic and prawns, sear 1 minute.
3. Push to one side, add tofu, and brown briefly.
4. Push everything aside, crack in eggs, let them set for 20 seconds, then break up.
5. Add drained noodles and pour in the sauce. Toss on high heat 2 minutes.
6. Add bean sprouts and spring onions. Toss 30 seconds.
7. Plate, top with crushed peanuts, and serve with lime wedges.

**Tips:**
- Soak noodles just until pliable — finish cooking in the wok.
- A hot wok and fast tossing is what gives the dish its character.

---

## Thai Green Curry (แกงเขียวหวาน)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/thai-green-curry-chicken

A fragrant coconut-milk curry with green chillies, lemongrass, and Thai basil — creamy, herbal, and bright with lime and galangal.

**Cuisine:** Thai (Thailand) · **Style:** Curry · **Protein:** Chicken
**Time:** 40 min total (15 prep + 25 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 3/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Non-Veg · Gluten-free · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 500g chicken thighs, sliced
- 3 tbsp Thai green curry paste
- 400ml coconut milk
- 1 cup chicken stock
- 8 Thai eggplants, quartered (or zucchini)
- 1 red bell pepper, sliced
- 1 tbsp fish sauce
- 1 tbsp palm sugar
- 4 kaffir lime leaves, torn
- 1 stalk lemongrass, bruised
- 1 handful Thai basil leaves
- 2 tbsp oil
- Red chilli slices to garnish

**Steps:**
1. Heat oil and fry curry paste on medium until fragrant, 2 minutes.
2. Pour in half the coconut milk and stir until oil separates on top.
3. Add chicken and sear for 4-5 minutes until sealed.
4. Pour in remaining coconut milk, stock, kaffir lime leaves, and lemongrass.
5. Add eggplants and bell pepper. Simmer 10-12 minutes until chicken is cooked through.
6. Season with fish sauce and palm sugar.
7. Stir in Thai basil just before serving.
8. Serve with jasmine rice, topped with chilli slices.

**Tips:**
- Splitting the coconut milk first gives a richer, glossier curry.
- Add basil at the very end so it stays fragrant.

---

## Tom Yum Goong (ต้มยำกุ้ง)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/tom-yum-goong

Thailand's legendary hot-and-sour prawn soup with lemongrass, galangal, kaffir lime, and chillies — sharp, bracing, and instantly warming.

**Cuisine:** Thai (Thailand) · **Style:** Soup · **Protein:** Prawn
**Time:** 35 min total (15 prep + 20 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 4/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Non-Veg · Gluten-free · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 400g large prawns, shells reserved
- 4 cups stock or water
- 2 stalks lemongrass, bruised and cut
- 4 slices galangal
- 6 kaffir lime leaves, torn
- 4 Thai bird's eye chillies, crushed
- 200g mushrooms (straw or button)
- 4 shallots, halved
- 3 tbsp fish sauce
- 3 tbsp lime juice
- 1 tbsp nam prik pao (chilli paste)
- 1/4 cup coriander leaves
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Simmer prawn shells in stock for 10 minutes for a deeper broth. Strain.
2. Return broth to the pot. Add lemongrass, galangal, kaffir lime leaves, chillies, and shallots.
3. Simmer 5 minutes to infuse.
4. Add mushrooms and prawns. Cook 2-3 minutes until prawns turn pink.
5. Turn off heat. Stir in fish sauce, lime juice, and nam prik pao.
6. Taste and balance — it should be sour first, then salty, then spicy.
7. Finish with coriander and serve immediately.

**Tips:**
- Add lime juice after turning off the heat or the soup turns bitter.
- Nam prik pao adds sweetness and body — worth seeking out.

---

## Chicken Teriyaki Donburi (照り焼き丼)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/chicken-teriyaki-donburi

Pan-seared chicken glazed in a sweet-savoury teriyaki sauce, sliced and served over steaming rice with scallions and sesame.

**Cuisine:** Japanese (Japan) · **Style:** Rice Bowl · **Protein:** Chicken
**Time:** 25 min total (10 prep + 15 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 2 · **Spice:** 1/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Non-Veg · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 2 boneless chicken thighs
- 3 tbsp soy sauce
- 2 tbsp mirin
- 2 tbsp sake
- 1 tbsp sugar
- 1 tsp grated ginger
- 1 tbsp oil
- 2 cups cooked short-grain rice
- 2 spring onions, sliced
- 1 tsp toasted sesame seeds
- Nori strips (optional)

**Steps:**
1. Whisk soy sauce, mirin, sake, sugar, and ginger into a teriyaki sauce.
2. Heat oil in a pan. Place chicken skin-side down and cook on medium for 6 minutes until deeply golden.
3. Flip and cook another 4-5 minutes until cooked through.
4. Pour off extra fat, add the sauce, and let it reduce 2 minutes until glossy and glazing the chicken.
5. Rest chicken 2 minutes, then slice.
6. Serve over a bowl of hot rice, drizzle over extra sauce, and top with spring onions, sesame, and nori.

**Tips:**
- Chicken thighs stay juicier than breasts for teriyaki — worth the swap.
- Reduce the sauce until it coats the back of a spoon for the signature glaze.

---

## Shoyu Ramen (醤油ラーメン)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/shoyu-ramen

A classic Tokyo-style ramen: springy noodles in a soy-sauce-seasoned chicken broth, topped with marinated egg, chashu, nori, and scallions.

**Cuisine:** Japanese (Japan) · **Style:** Soup · **Protein:** Chicken
**Time:** 60 min total (20 prep + 40 cook) · **Difficulty:** medium · **Servings:** 2 · **Spice:** 1/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Non-Veg · Contains egg · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 2 servings fresh ramen noodles
- 4 cups chicken stock
- 1/4 cup soy sauce
- 2 tbsp mirin
- 1 tbsp sake
- 1 tsp sugar
- 2 garlic cloves, smashed
- 1 inch ginger, sliced
- 2 soft-boiled eggs, halved
- 4 slices chashu pork or chicken
- 2 sheets nori
- 2 spring onions, sliced
- 1 tbsp toasted sesame oil
- Bamboo shoots and corn (optional)

**Steps:**
1. For the tare: simmer soy sauce, mirin, sake, sugar, garlic, and ginger for 5 minutes. Strain.
2. Heat chicken stock until just boiling, then keep hot.
3. Cook ramen noodles per packet instructions. Drain well.
4. Divide the tare between two bowls. Add a drizzle of sesame oil.
5. Pour hot stock over the tare and stir.
6. Nestle noodles into the bowl. Top with chashu, soft-boiled eggs, nori, spring onions, and bamboo shoots.
7. Serve immediately — ramen waits for no one.

**Tips:**
- Soft-boiled eggs: 6.5 minutes from boil, then straight into ice water.
- Balance the tare first in the empty bowl — it controls the final saltiness.

---

## Chicken Katsu Curry (カツカレー)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/katsu-curry

Crisp panko-crumbed chicken cutlet sliced over rice, bathed in Japanese curry sauce — a golden, nostalgic weeknight classic.

**Cuisine:** Japanese (Japan) · **Style:** Curry · **Protein:** Chicken
**Time:** 50 min total (20 prep + 30 cook) · **Difficulty:** medium · **Servings:** 2 · **Spice:** 1/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Non-Veg · Contains egg · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 2 boneless chicken breasts, flattened
- 1 egg, beaten
- 1/2 cup flour
- 1 cup panko breadcrumbs
- 1 onion, sliced
- 1 carrot, diced
- 1 potato, diced
- 2 garlic cloves, minced
- 1 tbsp grated ginger
- 2 cubes Japanese curry roux (or 2 tbsp curry powder + 2 tbsp butter + 2 tbsp flour)
- 3 cups chicken stock
- 1 tbsp soy sauce
- 1 tsp honey
- Cooked rice to serve
- Oil for frying
- Salt and pepper

**Steps:**
1. For the curry: sauté onion until golden, then add carrot, potato, garlic, and ginger. Cook 3 minutes.
2. Add stock and simmer 15 minutes until vegetables are tender.
3. Off heat, stir in curry roux cubes until dissolved. Add soy sauce and honey. Simmer 5 more minutes.
4. Season chicken and dredge in flour, egg, then panko.
5. Fry at 170°C for 5-6 minutes, turning once, until deep golden and cooked through.
6. Rest on a rack 2 minutes, then slice into strips.
7. Plate rice, spoon curry alongside, and top with sliced katsu.

**Tips:**
- Flatten the chicken evenly — it cooks through before the panko burns.
- Rest the katsu on a rack, not paper, to keep the crust crisp.

---

## Spaghetti Aglio e Olio (Spaghetti aglio e olio)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/spaghetti-aglio-e-olio

The Neapolitan pantry classic: spaghetti tossed with olive oil, slivered garlic, chilli, and parsley — five ingredients, one perfect dinner.

**Cuisine:** Italian (Campania) · **Style:** One Pot · **Protein:** Veggies
**Time:** 20 min total (5 prep + 15 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 2 · **Spice:** 2/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Vegan · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 200g spaghetti
- 6 garlic cloves, thinly sliced
- 1/3 cup good olive oil
- 1 tsp red chilli flakes
- 1/4 cup flat-leaf parsley, chopped
- Salt to taste
- Coarsely ground black pepper

**Steps:**
1. Boil spaghetti in heavily salted water until just shy of al dente. Reserve 1 cup pasta water.
2. Meanwhile, heat olive oil on low-medium. Add sliced garlic and cook gently 3-4 minutes until just golden — never brown.
3. Add chilli flakes off the heat.
4. Return pan to low heat. Add drained pasta and 1/2 cup pasta water.
5. Toss vigorously for 1-2 minutes until the sauce emulsifies and glazes the spaghetti.
6. Fold in parsley. Season with salt and pepper.
7. Serve immediately — no cheese, by tradition.

**Tips:**
- Don't let the garlic colour past pale gold or it turns bitter.
- The pasta water starch is what makes the oil cling to the noodles.

---

## Margherita Pizza (Pizza Margherita)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/margherita-pizza

The original Neapolitan pizza: blistered dough, crushed San Marzano tomato, fresh mozzarella, basil, and olive oil — red, white, and green for the Italian flag.

**Cuisine:** Italian (Campania) · **Style:** Baked · **Protein:** Paneer
**Time:** 35 min total (20 prep + 15 cook) · **Difficulty:** medium · **Servings:** 2 · **Spice:** 1/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner, snacks · **Dietary:** Vegetarian

**Ingredients:**
- 300g pizza dough (rested 24 hours)
- 1/2 cup crushed San Marzano tomatoes
- 150g fresh mozzarella (fior di latte), torn
- 10 fresh basil leaves
- 2 tbsp olive oil
- Semolina for dusting
- Flaky sea salt

**Steps:**
1. Heat oven to its maximum (ideally 280°C) with a pizza stone or steel inside for 30 minutes.
2. On a semolina-dusted surface, gently stretch dough into a 12-inch round, leaving a puffy edge.
3. Season tomato with a pinch of salt and a splash of olive oil.
4. Spread tomato thinly over the dough, leaving the border clean.
5. Dot torn mozzarella across the surface.
6. Slide onto the hot stone and bake 6-9 minutes until crust is leopard-spotted and cheese is bubbling.
7. Remove, drizzle olive oil, scatter basil, and finish with flaky salt.
8. Slice and serve hot.

**Tips:**
- Too much topping makes the centre soggy — restraint is everything.
- A pizza steel in a home oven gets closer to a wood-fired base than a stone.

---

## Chicken Parmigiana (Pollo alla parmigiana)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/chicken-parmigiana

Crisp breaded chicken cutlets topped with tomato sauce, mozzarella, and parmesan, then baked until golden and bubbling — hearty Italian-American comfort food.

**Cuisine:** Italian (Italy) · **Style:** Grilled · **Protein:** Chicken
**Time:** 50 min total (20 prep + 30 cook) · **Difficulty:** medium · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 1/5
**Meal:** dinner · **Dietary:** Non-Veg · Contains egg

**Ingredients:**
- 4 chicken breasts, butterflied and flattened
- 2 eggs, beaten
- 1 cup flour
- 1.5 cups panko or fine breadcrumbs
- 1/2 cup grated parmesan
- 2 cups marinara sauce
- 200g fresh mozzarella, sliced
- 1/4 cup olive oil
- Fresh basil leaves
- Salt and pepper

**Steps:**
1. Season chicken with salt and pepper.
2. Mix breadcrumbs with half the parmesan. Set up a dredge: flour, egg, breadcrumbs.
3. Coat each cutlet and shallow fry in olive oil until deeply golden on both sides, about 3 minutes per side.
4. Preheat oven to 200°C. Spread marinara in a baking dish.
5. Place fried chicken on the sauce, top each with more marinara, mozzarella, and remaining parmesan.
6. Bake 12-15 minutes until cheese is melted and bubbling.
7. Rest 3 minutes, scatter basil, and serve with spaghetti or a green salad.

**Tips:**
- Flatten chicken evenly so the crust stays crisp while the centre cooks through.
- Don't drown the cutlets — a crisp edge around the sauce is part of the appeal.

---

## Mushroom Risotto (Risotto ai funghi)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/mushroom-risotto-italian

Creamy Arborio rice cooked slowly with mushrooms, white wine, and parmesan — the quintessential Northern Italian primo.

**Cuisine:** Italian (Lombardy) · **Style:** One Pot · **Protein:** Veggies
**Time:** 45 min total (15 prep + 30 cook) · **Difficulty:** medium · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 1/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Vegetarian · Gluten-free

**Ingredients:**
- 1.5 cups Arborio rice
- 400g mixed mushrooms (cremini, shiitake, oyster)
- 1 small onion, finely diced
- 2 garlic cloves, minced
- 1/2 cup dry white wine
- 5 cups warm vegetable stock
- 1/2 cup grated parmesan
- 3 tbsp butter
- 2 tbsp olive oil
- 2 tbsp fresh thyme leaves
- Salt and pepper
- Extra parmesan and parsley to serve

**Steps:**
1. Sauté mushrooms in olive oil on high heat until deeply golden. Season with thyme, salt, and pepper. Set aside.
2. In the same pan, melt 1 tbsp butter. Soften onion and garlic on low.
3. Add rice and toast 2 minutes until edges turn translucent.
4. Pour in wine and stir until absorbed.
5. Add warm stock one ladle at a time, stirring constantly. Wait for each to absorb before adding more. About 18 minutes total.
6. When rice is al dente and creamy, fold in mushrooms, remaining butter, and parmesan.
7. Rest 2 minutes off heat, then serve with more parmesan and parsley.

**Tips:**
- Keep the stock warm — cold stock shocks the rice and stalls the cook.
- Finish with cold butter off-heat for the signature glossy mantecatura.

---

## Chicken Shawarma (شاورما الدجاج)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/chicken-shawarma

Lebanese-style chicken marinated in warm spices, roasted until crisp at the edges, and wrapped in pita with garlic toum and pickles.

**Cuisine:** Middle Eastern (Levant) · **Style:** Grilled · **Protein:** Chicken
**Time:** 45 min total (20 prep + 25 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 2/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner, snacks · **Dietary:** Non-Veg

**Ingredients:**
- 750g boneless chicken thighs
- 1/4 cup yogurt
- 3 tbsp olive oil
- 3 tbsp lemon juice
- 4 garlic cloves, minced
- 2 tsp cumin
- 2 tsp coriander powder
- 2 tsp smoked paprika
- 1 tsp turmeric
- 1 tsp cinnamon
- 1/2 tsp allspice
- 1/2 tsp cardamom
- Salt and pepper
- 4 pita breads
- Toum (garlic sauce) or tahini
- Pickles, tomato, and lettuce to serve

**Steps:**
1. Whisk yogurt, olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, and all spices into a marinade.
2. Coat chicken and marinate at least 2 hours, preferably overnight.
3. Heat a grill pan or oven to 220°C.
4. Sear chicken 4-5 minutes per side until deeply charred and cooked through.
5. Rest 5 minutes, then slice thinly against the grain.
6. Warm pita. Smear with toum, layer chicken, pickles, tomato, and lettuce.
7. Wrap tight and serve with extra toum and hot sauce.

**Tips:**
- Thighs stay juicy under the char — don't use breast for shawarma.
- Chopping the cooked chicken small before serving recreates the spit-roasted texture.

---

## Falafel (فلافل)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/falafel

Crisp chickpea-and-herb fritters, deep green inside and deeply golden outside — the iconic Middle Eastern street food.

**Cuisine:** Middle Eastern (Levant) · **Style:** Fried · **Protein:** Lentils
**Time:** 35 min total (20 prep + 15 cook) · **Difficulty:** medium · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 2/5
**Meal:** snacks, lunch · **Dietary:** Vegan · Gluten-free · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 1.5 cups dried chickpeas (soaked overnight, not cooked)
- 1 small onion
- 4 garlic cloves
- 1 cup flat-leaf parsley
- 1/2 cup coriander leaves
- 1/4 cup dill (optional)
- 1 tsp cumin
- 1 tsp coriander powder
- 1 tsp baking soda
- 1 tbsp sesame seeds
- Oil for deep frying
- Salt and pepper
- Pita, tahini, pickles, and tomato to serve

**Steps:**
1. Drain soaked (uncooked) chickpeas well.
2. Pulse chickpeas, onion, garlic, herbs, cumin, coriander, salt, and pepper in a food processor until coarse and bright green.
3. Rest the mixture 20 minutes in the fridge.
4. Stir in baking soda and sesame seeds.
5. Shape into small patties or quenelles.
6. Deep fry at 170°C in batches for 3-4 minutes until deep brown and crisp.
7. Drain and serve immediately in warm pita with tahini, pickles, and tomato.

**Tips:**
- Never use canned or cooked chickpeas — only soaked raw ones will hold together.
- Fry one test falafel first; adjust salt and binding before doing the rest.

---

## Fattoush (فتوش)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/fattoush

A bright Levantine salad of romaine, cucumber, tomato, herbs, and crisp toasted pita, dressed with sumac, lemon, and olive oil.

**Cuisine:** Middle Eastern (Levant) · **Style:** Light · **Protein:** Veggies
**Time:** 25 min total (15 prep + 10 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 1/5
**Meal:** lunch · **Dietary:** Vegan · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 2 pita breads
- 1 romaine lettuce, chopped
- 2 tomatoes, chopped
- 1 cucumber, diced
- 4 radishes, sliced
- 1/2 red onion, thinly sliced
- 1/2 cup flat-leaf parsley
- 1/2 cup mint leaves
- 1/3 cup olive oil
- 3 tbsp lemon juice
- 1 tbsp pomegranate molasses
- 2 tsp sumac
- 1 garlic clove, grated
- Salt and pepper

**Steps:**
1. Split pita, brush with olive oil, and bake or fry until deeply crisp. Break into shards.
2. Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, pomegranate molasses, sumac, garlic, salt, and pepper into a dressing.
3. In a large bowl, combine lettuce, tomatoes, cucumber, radishes, onion, parsley, and mint.
4. Just before serving, drizzle the dressing and toss.
5. Scatter the pita shards on top and dust with more sumac.
6. Serve immediately while the pita is still crisp.

**Tips:**
- Add the pita at the very end so it stays crunchy.
- Pomegranate molasses gives the signature tang — worth buying.
